title: Centrifuge Report of Normal vs Dermatitis Skin Microbiome
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"fjum3","text":"Centrifuge is a classification software used on whole genome shotgun (WGS) reads. It uses a reference database to determine what organisms are found in your sample. This protocol will show you how to access the data on CyVerse and walk you through some questions to help in the analysis of the plots. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Log into your account on the CyVerse Discovery Environment (click link below). https://de.cyverse.org/de/
Click on the data icon found on the left hand side of the Discovery Environment. a
There are two files you need to download, normvsderm.png and dermatitis.csv. Check mark both those files and click Download in the menu. Click "Simple Download" a (Clicking will enlarge image)
A new box will open with two links. Click both those links to download the files. a

normvsderm.png is a bubble plot of proportions of organisms found in the samples. There are a total of 6 samples, 3 had dermatitis (red) and 3 were normal (blue). The size of the dot indicates the proportion of the classified data that matched to that organism with larger dots meaning a higher proportion and smaller ones a lower proportion.
Do you see any patterns? Are there any organisms that appear in all of the samples? Do any of the samples have unique organisms?
Are there any patterns between the Dermatitis and Normal samples?
dermatitis.csv is the data used to generate the plot.
creator: James E Thornton Jr
